_summary:
ONMYOJI BATTLE MECHANICS OVERVIEW (Global/EN).

TURN ORDER (SPEED / MOVE BAR): Onmyoji is a turn-based RPG. Turn order is decided by the Move Bar (action bar). Each unit advances along the bar based on its Speed (SPD) stat; a unit with twice the speed acts roughly twice as often. Progress resets each round. 'First speed' = whoever moves first on your team; 'outspeed' (超车) = being faster than the opponent. Skills can apply Speed effects (change the SPD stat) or Progress/Pull/Push effects (move a unit's bar position by a percentage, e.g. Yamausagi/Kamaitachi 'pullers'). Once a unit reaches its own turn it cannot lose it even if pushed. Anti-interrupt formula: Second Speed Min = First Speed x (100% - Pull Amount), or First Speed / (100% + Push Amount).

DAMAGE TYPES: Direct damage scales off the attacker's Attack (most common) or Health (HP-scaling shikigami). True Damage ignores the target's Defense and deals exactly the intended amount (also can carry attacker soul/ATK buffs and target debuffs; reflected damage behaves as true damage). Indirect Damage comes from stacked, timed status effects (e.g. Poison); it is guaranteed (ignores accuracy/resist), works on immune bosses, does not trigger Mitama/soul effects, cannot be negated, but is affected by attacker crit and target defense (guaranteed crit if target Def=0). Poison: dispellable debuff with 9 levels; lowers target SPD by 10% and ignores (level x 10) defense on indirect damage.

DAMAGE FORMULA: Final Damage = Skill Attack x (300 / (300 + Total Defense)) x (1 + Damage Buff - Mitigation Buff) x (1 + Debuff). Defense gives diminishing returns via the 300/(300+DEF) term and can never go below 0. Random floats make damage vary slightly. PVP 'judgement flag': damage is roughly doubled and healing roughly halved every three rounds.

CRIT (CRITICAL): Crit Rate (暴击) is chance to land a critical hit; Crit Damage (暴击伤害) is the bonus multiplier on a crit. 'Full/max crit' (满暴) means crit rate at or near 100%. Core offensive stats ('three sizes' 三围) in soul slots 2/4/6 are SPD / ATK / Crit(DMG). Crit also affects shield strength and heals.

EFFECT HIT vs EFFECT RESIST (control/accuracy mechanics): Control effects and resistance are calculated separately. When a control is applied it is first checked whether it lands at all (Accuracy), then whether it is resisted (Resistance). Accuracy Rate = Base Accuracy x (1 + Accuracy[Effect Hit]). Control Success Rate = Base Accuracy x (1 + Accuracy) / (1 + Resistance[Effect Resist]). So higher Effect Hit raises landing chance, higher Effect Resist lowers it.

CONTROL EFFECTS (status that stop a unit acting): Daze/Stun (眩晕), Sleep (沉睡), Freeze (冰冻), Deep Freeze (深度冰冻), Confuse/Chaos (混乱), Taunt/Provoke (嘲罵 - forces target to normal-attack the source), Silence (沈黙), Seal (封印 - blocks special skills), Bind/Confine/Prison (禁锢), Morph/Transform, and Provocation. A unit is 'Immobile' when unable to take a turn (Banish, Freeze, Deep Freeze, Sleep, Daze, Transform). Some controls are dispellable, others (Morph, Daze, Provoke, Deep Freeze, Shadowbound, Fettered, Ruined, Rest in Ice) are not. Effect categories: Status (dispellable, no stack), Mark (印, non-dispellable, stackable), Realm (結界, ally buff aura), Field (幻境, battlefield aura).

ORBS / ONIBI (鬼火) - resource for skills: Blue will-o'-wisps consumed to cast special skills. The orb bar holds max 8 orbs; players start with 4. The bar fills by 1 segment per turn; when full it grants orbs (3, then 4, then max 5 after filling 3 times). Starting orbs can be raised by Azure Basan / Zashiki Warashi; fill speed by Oitsukigami. Some shikigami/souls grant, steal, or block orbs. Special orb types: Wish Power (SP Kaguya - substitutes for missing onibi) and Faux Orbs (Kotodama - replaces enemy onibi and is consumed first).

ONMYOJI (PLAYER UNITS): Onmyoji are the protagonist player characters, technically SSR rarity, with their own attribute/skill set distinct from shikigami. Their special skills are called 'onmyoudo' (陰陽道) vs shikigami 'demonic arts' (妖術). Unlocked via main story; all share growth data except skills.

SHIELDS: Absorb damage as a stand-in HP buffer; surrounding shields block most soul effects and status effects (poison, bloodsucking) but not most skill effects or true damage. Shield HP scales off creator's ATK/HP plus crit. Dispel skills remove shields.
Formulae:
Some calculations are not official.

Attribute Addition:

\begin{align}
Total\ Attribute & = Base \\
& + Mitama\ Flat\ Addition \\
& + Base \times Mitama\ Percentage \\
& + Attribute\ Strengthening \\
& + Base \times (1 + Status\ Effects) \\
\end{align}

Also apply flat attribute addition if need. Some attributes are subject to critical effects as well.

Skill Effect:

\begin{align}
Skill\ Effect & = Total\ Attribute \\
& \times Skill\ Effect \times (1 + Skill\ Modifiers) \\
& \times (1 + Mitama\ Effects) \\
\end{align}

Damage Calculation:
There are random floats involved so damage is not completely the same.

\begin{align}
Final\ Damage & = Skill\ Attack \times \left ( \frac{300}{300 + Total\ Defense} \right ) \\
& \times (1 + Damage\ Buff - Mitigation\ Buff) \times (1 + Debuff) \\
\end{align}

Action bar calculation:
To not be interrupted by opposing side:

Second\ Speed\ Minimum = First\ Speed \times ( 100%\ - Pull\ Amount)
or
Second\ Speed\ Minimum = First\ Speed \div ( 100%\ + Push\ Amount)

Derivations:

Last\ Speed\ Minimum = First\ Speed \times ( 100%\ - First\ Pull - Second\ Pull)

First\ Speed\ Minimum = Last\ Speed \div ( 100%\ - First\ Pull - Second\ Pull)

Speed addition like Oitsuki has yet to be investigated.

Judgement flag calculation:
Damage is approximately doubled every three rounds, and heal is approximately halved.

Control calculation:

Control and resist are calculated separately. When control effect is released, first it is calculated if it will take effect at all, then it is calculated if the effect will be resisted.

Control chance:

Accuracy\ Rate = Base\ Accuracy \times (1+Accuracy)

Control chance against resist:

Control\ Success\ Rate = Base\ Accuracy \times \frac{(1+Accuracy)}{(1 + Resistance)}

Control\ Failure\ Rate = 1-Inaccurate\ Rate-Success\ Rate

Derivations:

Total\ Control\ Success\ Rate = Accuracy\ Rate \times \frac{(Resistance)}{(1 + Resistance)}

Total\ Control\ Failure\ Rate = 1 - Total\ Control\ Success\ Rate

Delegati
Move Bar:
The bar that dictates the order in which units have their turns.

Progress:
Progress refers to how a unit moves from the beginning to the end of the action bar. It is affected by the speed of the unit. A unit with speed that is twice as high can move twice as often than another unit. In other words, the length of the bar appears to be be determined by the speeds of units.

Progress is reset across rounds - a larger concept of "battle."

Manipulation of Progress:

Speed Effects:
Some skills will grant higher or lower speed to targets, so the move order would be affected.

Progress Effects:
Some skills can affect the progression of units on a percentage basis. No immediate effects of progression will happen on the unit's own turn (i.e. Kamaitachi, Yamakaze with passive trigger), but even shikigami on their own turns can pull themselves so that they return to have their turn earlier after the current turn. However, once units arrive on their own turn (different from 100% progression), there is no way for them to lose their turn even if push effect is incurred.

Note that Kagura and Yokinshi's skill falls into this category, so the target, even with 100% progress, has chance to be pushed back from effects like Juzu.

Battle:
A battle lasts from the entering and exiting of instances. Typically, from seeing the lineup interface, to the reward calculation interface.

Round:

PVE:
A round is a stage of a battle, and all status effects, cooldowns and the action bar is reset when a new round begins. The HP and orb counts stay the same. Some shikigami skills can span rounds.

PVP:
A round passes when all units of one side have taken their turn (moving is irrelevant). The judgement flag bestows buffs and debuffs based on the number of rounds that have passed.

Turn:
Onmyoji is a turn-based RPG. It is a mechanism for battle. Closely associated with "move." See also "round."

There is real turn and false turn.

Real Turn:
A real turn is when the unit has chance to move and player
Damage:
Damage is the mechanism of how units in the game lose health and are ultimately defeated (with the exception of some boss-related specials like instant death).

Direct Damage:

Attack Damage:
Damage is dealt based on the attacker's attack.

Health Damage:
Damage is dealt based on the attacker's health.

True Damage:
True damage is damage that, for whatever reason, can ignore the target's defense, and deal damage exactly equivalent to what is supposed to be dealt without defense.

However, true damage also may convey the attacker's soul/attack-related status effects and the target's negative status effects, to increase their damage.

Reflected damage appears to be true damage.

Indirect Damage:
Rules
A type of damage, damage that is not considered indirect will be considered direct damage.
Damage is dealt from stacked status effects on enemy that last for some number of turns, and stacking is guaranteed, irrespective of accuracy or resist, and works on immune bosses as well; without further specifications, indirect damage statuses are usually dispellable.
Qualities
Damage does not trigger mitama effects on either side.
Damage cannot be negated by other skills or effects (i.e. link, Teikon, Kingyo).
When damage is calculated, will be affected by the attacker's crit/damage and target's defense. Specifically, damage is guaranteed to crit if target has 0 defense.
Further, the lowest an unit's defense can be is 0, it cannot be negative

Poison:
A type of debuff status, can be dispelled, and is affected by accuracy and resistance. It has 9 levels, and has the effect of lowering the target's speed by 10%, as well as ignoring target's status level * 10 defense. Various levels of poison statuses' defense ignoring effects can stack onto one another, but speed lowering is maintained to be the same. Thus, the levels are:
Lower target's speed by 10%, and when calculating indirect damage ignore 10 defense
Lower target's speed by 10%, and when calculating indirect damage ignore 20
Skill Effects:
Skills are the various abilities of characters in the game.

They are called "demonic arts" (妖術) when referring to special skills of shikigami, and the skills of onmyouji are called "onmyoudo" (陰陽道).

Types:
Under auto mode, two types of skills can be toggled: normal attacks and special skills. Normal attacks are skill 1 and consume no onibi. Special skills tend to be skill 3, but can be skill 2 as well, and have effects other than simply dealing damage.

Certain skills have a chance to automatically activate without consuming any onibi nor needing any controls once their requirements are met. These skills are usually called passives, which tend to be skill 2, however it is possible for normal and special skills to have similar properties.

Properties:
Skills have unique names, icons, and voiced lines from their correlating characters.

Skill effects can be either shikigami-specific or use a common mechanism. Specific skill mechanisms are denoted in red text, while common effects appear in blue text.

Shield:
Shields are created using skills or soul effects which prevents health loss to a unit by acting as a target in the unit's place, absorbing damage up until the limit of the shield's health, at which point the unit will begin taking damage.

Effects:
A shield that surrounds the target will negate most soul effects, but most skill effects cannot be blocked. If a shield only partially absorbs damage, then soul effect may trigger on that leftover damage. This is similar in reverse, some soul effects on a target, such as Senecio, Mirror Lady, or Scarlet, will not trigger as the target is not taking damage. Likewise, units with skills that trigger on being attacked (not damaged) are not affected when shielded.

Status effects such as poison or bloodsucking will be blocked.

Since Seimei's shield does not surround the target but becomes a new target, it has unique properties such as being immune to control, health being replenished, and blocking skill as well as soul effects. It will not, however, block targetted damage such as Shoyo's curse or Higanbana's passive.

Dispel:
Unless otherwise noted, dispel skills can remove shields. The damage, if any, from the dispel will be absorbed first, and then shield will disappear.

Damage:
The health of a shield, aside from being based on the shield creator's health or attack etc., depends on the critical rate and critical damage of the creator, and in the case of Tomb Guard, shield thickness also depends on equipped soul. It is unclear whether the critical rate is applied on shield creation or when shield is attacked, which is why Pearl is said to not involve crit - even though its shield will have more health if the heal crits.

The damage that a shield can withstand depends on the target's defense, which is factored into the damage equation. Equippers with souls like Boroboroton will also decrease the damage taken by shield.

Shields cannot prevent true damage (Diamond Sutra, Mirror Lady, Water Circuit) to target, as they are targ
CN Terminology:
Various CN terms used by players in Onmyouji. Definitions from elf. Not all are original to Onmyoji.

Game Terminology:
猜拳 (rock paper scissors)
When a shikigami doesn't have max crit so they could either crit for a greater heal/dps or not do it at all (usually exactly when you need the crit too)
超车 (outspeed)
When your first speed is faster than your opponent so you move first
赌魂 (soul gambling)
When you get souls from the shop, scales or the shrine
三围 (three sizes)
SPD/ATK/Crit (DMG) in slots 2, 4 and 6
满暴 (full crit/max crit)
Crit reaches 100% or very close to 100%
压级 (level suppress)
Purposefully not levelling up in order to have easier raids and/or satisfaction at beating higher-levelled players
魂十一 (s11/soul 11)
An old term. Used to refer to getting souls from the shop
魂土 (s12/soul 12)
Refers to Fallen Sun
低保 (minimum wage)
Getting the bare minimum in duels each week so as to not fall in rank
双速 (double speed)
when a slot 2 soul has both speed as main stat and substat
反喂 (reverse feed)
Feeding a shikigami that has been fed skills already to another shikigami of the same type. The feeder's skill points will be redistributed so it's a common practice to kamaitachis that have beat up lv5
毕业 (graduate)
Level 40 and the best souls possible for that particular shikigami
一拖四 (carry 4)
When one strong shikigami carries four weak ones, usually to gain EXP
尬舞天团 (dance troupe)
3 or 4 yamausagis/kamaitachis, as they can constantly pull each other and prevent the opponent from moving. So named because they constantly rabbit dance/wiggle wiggle wiggle
一速 (first speed)
Whoever moves first in your team is called this
拉条 (pull/puller)
Shikigami who increase your action bar, most often referring to Yami Usagi and Kamaitachi
PVE
Player vs Environment, generally player control vs AI
一波流 (one hit comp)
Compositions that seek to take out the enemy's core unit in one wave of concentrated attacks
反手 (backhand comp)
Compositions that seek to survive enemy attacks and wear the opposing
Onmyoji:
This article is about the titular characters, for more information about the game, see Game
Onmyoji are the protagonists of the story, and are units that have a different set of attributes and skills compared to shikigami or yokai.

Onmyoji are technically of SSR rarity.

Introduction:
Onmyoji are special individuals that help to bridge the world of humans and spirits. Evidently, some are "evil" as will be revealed later in the story.

Onmyoji are unlocked by passing various points of the main story, and there are currently four so far. All onmyoji share the same data for growth, with the exception of their skills.

Levelling:

Category:Data
Orbs:
Orbs, or Onibi (鬼火), are the blue will o' the wisps that a majority of shikigami consume in order to use their special skills, the number consumed varies based on the skills and active effects in certain content.

Mechanics:
At the bottom of the battle interface is the orb bar, or onibi bar, which can hold a maximum of 8 orbs. By default, players start with 4 orbs. Orbs are gained by filling up the orb bar and it fills up by 1 bar each turn. To the right of bar is a number with a plus sign that shows how many orbs will be obtained when the bar is filled. The number of orbs that can be gained from orb bar increases from 3 to 4 and then to a maximum of 5 after it has been filled 3 times.

The starting number of orbs can be increased by Azure Basan, Zashiki Warashi and active effects in events.

How quickly the orb bar fills up can be increased by shikigami such as Oitsukigami and SP Oitsukigami.

Outside of natural orb generation with the orb bar, some shikigami and souls can grant additional orbs. Similarly, some can also remove/steal/replace or increase the cost to use skills for enemy teams.
